text: Inbound via self-serve signup (used initial free credits). Product-led trial: created API key, ran basic text-gen for ticket replies. CTO (Rina Patel) + Head of Support (Marco Reyes) on initial call. Key asks: fast, structured JSON outputs for triage system; tool-calling to create/update tickets in their internal ticketing microservice; server-side ticket summarization to include "summary", "priority_hint", "possible_tags" fields. Latency: P95 target <= 600ms for agent-assist flows; throughput typical 20-50 req/min, bursts up to 200 req/min during morning surge. Cost sensitivity: high — SMB budget, wants predictable per-request pricing and guidance on batching/kv-caching to reduce token cost. Model preference: open models or Redwood-curated smaller models to control cost; open to Redwood recommendations. Security: requires SSO (SAML), audit logs retention 90 days, KMS for keys. Quote ...

text:
Inbound SMB trial. Fast signup; self-serve. Created first key but usage stalled after smoke test. Key facts:
- Primary contact: Thomas Reed (CTO, small engineering team).
- Use-case: searchable product docs + in-app support assistant. Wants embeddings + chat routing as next step.
- Latency target: 200-350ms p95 for short chat flows; throughput low (10-30 qps peak).
- Cost sensitivity: high — looking to minimize per-token spend; asked about batching + prefix caching.
- Model preference: open models OK if cost/latency competitive; asked about Redwood-verified low-cost variants.
- Security: basic SOC2 assurance acceptable for now; will escalate to SSO/SAML if they move to Dedicated.
Challenges/observations:
- After initial SE call, no real requests beyond healthcheck. Likely cause: team started evaluating OpenAI due to aggressive free trial credits and ecosystem plugins.
- Quote from Tho...

text:
CTO (Aisha Rahman): 'Need sub-200ms p95 for chat streaming during promotional peaks'

POC scope: 2-week hosted run with synthetic traffic pattern (normal + 3x spike window). Measure end-to-end streaming p95 and tool-calling latency to Returns API.

SE notes: plan to enable prefix caching and small continuous batching; evaluate quantization recommendations from Redwood Optimize if token cost too high.

Procurement note: finance wants committed pricing for Dedicated before Q3 planning; may push to procurement if POC shows unstable p95 under simulated peaks.

Security: legal flagged PCI adjacency for returns workflows (card last4 in logs) -> asking about redaction + retention controls. KMS and audit logs required.

Integration detail: tool-calling will hit internal orders service via VPC connector; they need SAML SSO + SCIM for users.

Action items: run hosted POC, collect p95 for stream...

title: Copper Fern Digital
preview: Inbound SMB lead from self-serve signup. Team: 4 engineers, 1 product owner. Primary goal: embed streaming chat into merchant support console with tool-calls to inventory/returns microservices and strict structured JSON for downstream order updates. Initial demo showed rapid latency and token cost wins on hosted API...
text:
Inbound SMB lead from self-serve signup. Team: 4 engineers, 1 product owner. Primary goal: embed streaming chat into merchant support console with tool-calls to inventory/returns microservices and strict structured JSON for downstream order updates. Initial demo showed rapid latency and token cost wins on hosted API. 

Key pain reported: using 'JSON mode' (prompt-based) they get valid output most of the time, but in streaming mode tokens occasionally include trailing commentary or partial arrays that fail client JSON.parse. They also tried function calling (function_call:auto) but their function signatures differ slightly (optional fields, snake_case vs camelCase) and client SDK errors are bubbled up and confuse front-end handlers.

Customer quote (PM): "When streaming drops a stray "" or newline we immediately blow up our parsers — not acceptable for order update flows."

Dev notes: ...

title: Aurum Horizon Systems
preview:
Summary: Aurum Horizon operates route-optimization and real-time ETA services for large commercial fleets. They're evaluating Redwood Dedicated with multi-region reserved pools to guarantee throughput during peak routing windows and to meet contractual latency SLOs for enterprise customers.

Key customer quotes / sn...
text:
Summary: Aurum Horizon operates route-optimization and real-time ETA services for large commercial fleets. They're evaluating Redwood Dedicated with multi-region reserved pools to guarantee throughput during peak routing windows and to meet contractual latency SLOs for enterprise customers.

Key customer quotes / snippets:
- 'We need predictable throughput for morning/evening load spikes — can't have degraded routing for 10k+ vessels.'
- 'Failover between us-east-1 and us-west-2 must be seamless; 15–20% headroom required.'

POC observations (short):
- Baseline (2-day): 120 reqs/s sustained on a mixed batch profile (avg payload 400 tokens); 95th pct latency for generation ~140ms using baseline A100 configs.
- Observed token distribution skewed: 60% short ( <128 ), 30% medium (128–512), 10% long (512–2048) — affects batching/windowing.

Commercial / procurement notes:
- Want reserved GP...

title: Oxley Threadlytics
preview: Background: Oxley Threadlytics is a mid-market customer support platform used by ~300 customers (help centers, SMB to enterprise). They want to add an automated summarization + tagging layer to reduce agent handle time and improve routing. Primary integrations: Zendesk and Freshdesk via webhook/Apps. Key asks: high-...
text:
Background: Oxley Threadlytics is a mid-market customer support platform used by ~300 customers (help centers, SMB to enterprise). They want to add an automated summarization + tagging layer to reduce agent handle time and improve routing. Primary integrations: Zendesk and Freshdesk via webhook/Apps. Key asks: high-fidelity 1-3 sentence summaries for long ticket threads, consistent auto-tags (product, urgency, channel), and macros/tool-calls to trigger refunds/NRR flows. They emphasized reliability during Black Friday-like spikes — need predictable autoscaling and graceful degradation (fallback to a smaller model or simple heuristics) rather than failures.

POC scope (agreed in call): 2-week pilot ingesting 5k anonymized tickets, enable real-time webhook path from Zendesk, run batch backfill for 90 days of tickets, evaluate: tag precision >= 85%, summary usefulness >= 4/5 by product t...

title: Ventra AuditFlow Inc.

text:
Initial enterprise inbound for Private VPC + per-tenant KMS. Heavy compliance focus (EEA residency + 7y retention for audit logs). Customer repeatedly: "We cannot export raw prompts outside EU or to third-party SaaS without DPA". Security team wants SIEM integration with Azure Sentinel (CEF or JSON over Event Hub), Splunk as secondary. Key ask: complete log schema and retention SLA for legal and infosec review.

Summary bullets:
- Procurement stage, commercial review started 2026-02.
- Security review kickoff 2026-02-17 (Fireflies ff_2026-02-17_ventra-demo).
- POC request: one-week private deployment in their EU VNet, dedicated HSM-backed KMS key, SIEM export enabled.
- They require request/response access logs (metadata only) + structured audit events for policy decisions.
- Redaction rule: remove full prompt text; store prompt_hash + sampling mechanism for QA (hashed + admin-access ...

title: Lombard Harbor Research Vault
preview: Summary: Lombard Harbor is a buy-side research platform (equities + fixed income) building a unified knowledge portal for analysts and desk apps. Primary need: large-scale RAG search with embeddings + reranking across proprietary research, regulatory filings, vendor datasets (~40M documents target index). Preference...
text:
Summary: Lombard Harbor is a buy-side research platform (equities + fixed income) building a unified knowledge portal for analysts and desk apps. Primary need: large-scale RAG search with embeddings + reranking across proprietary research, regulatory filings, vendor datasets (~40M documents target index). Preference for Redwood Dedicated with VPC + customer KMS/HSM for key separation. Key asks: predictable latency for reranking (target 150-250ms end-to-end for top-k), embeddings throughput for nightly rebuilds (500k+ docs/day), and clear cost per query modeling (goal <$0.01 avg/query for common QA flows).

Security/Compliance: must support SSO/SAML, audit logging, per-tenant data residency (NA + EU), KMS with HSM-backed keys, retention controls and log export. Procurement/Legal: want SOC2/ISO evidence and a stronger data-residency SLA.

Quote from CTO on 2026-02-12 call: "We cannot ac...

title: CedarPoint Support Labs
preview: Goal: customer-support RAG system to deflect tickets and feed agent assist. Must index ~0.5M–4M historical tickets + KB, support incremental ingestion, and deliver top-5 retrieval + reranking within 200ms p95. Prefer a two-stage architecture: ANN coarse retriever (HNSW/IVF) + cross-encoder or lightweight transformer...
text: Goal: customer-support RAG system to deflect tickets and feed agent assist. Must index ~0.5M–4M historical tickets + KB, support incremental ingestion, and deliver top-5 retrieval + reranking within 200ms p95. Prefer a two-stage architecture: ANN coarse retriever (HNSW/IVF) + cross-encoder or lightweight transformer reranker for top-5 quality. Cost sensitivity: target <$0.005/query at current traffic; willing to pay for Dedicated for predictable latency. Security: VPC deployment, SSO, audit logs, KMS for keys, and retention controls. Must support SSO provisioning and produce an architecture diagram for infosec review.

title: Helio Financial
preview: Enterprise requirements centered on auditability and safe operations for route-level experiments. Helio is comfortable with Redwood’s route-level A/B testing concept, but they will not run it on production payment-adjacent workflows without (1) complete audit logging for experiment lifecycle and configuration change...
text: Enterprise requirements centered on auditability and safe operations for route-level experiments. Helio is comfortable with Redwood’s route-level A/B testing concept, but they will not run it on production payment-adjacent workflows without (1) complete audit logging for experiment lifecycle and configuration changes, (2) RBAC separation of duties, and (3) export to SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls. Secondary requirements: deterministic stickiness for end-user assignment and guardrails to auto-stop/soft-stop on latency/error regressions.

text:
Account summary
- Security-focused prospect building an internal-facing “analyst copilot” + customer-facing incident narrative generation. Current stack is mixed: some workloads on Bedrock, some DIY (vLLM) for cost control.
- Primary evaluation driver: auditability + predictable unit economics. Very sensitive to observability gaps.

What they care about (verbatim-ish)
- “We need to explain why the bill moved.” They want cache efficiency telemetry to be usable for internal chargeback by product area (route) and model version.
- “Audit logs can’t be an afterthought.” Needs clear event history for config changes, access, and deployment/rollout actions.

Product interest
- Redwood Private (VPC) is the lead option. Dedicated capacity is a backup if VPC timeline slips.
- Optimize add-on is attractive if recommendations are explainable and safe (no black-box auto-tuning).

text:
Account summary
- Regulated healthcare system (mix of hospitals + outpatient). Evaluating Redwood primarily for Private deployment due to PHI handling + network controls.
- Current pain: internal platform team is stuck between (a) too-broad admin roles in existing tooling, and (b) brittle per-service IAM policies. They want consistent least-privilege, with strong audit trails for “who changed what” and “why access was denied.”

Workload / technical context
- Use cases under consideration:
 - Clinician-facing note summarization (high sensitivity; strict data residency + retention)
 - Internal knowledge base search over policies/procedures (embeddings + reranking)
 - Support agent assist for scheduling + billing (chat + tool calling)
- Performance: “latency matters but predictability matters more.” Target p95 for chat tokens < ~250ms/token streaming on internal network; embeddings throu...

text:
Summary: mid-market quant trading platform; heavy concentration on sub-40ms p50 inference for chat assistants and sub-10ms reranking paths. Extremely cost-sensitive at scale — want predictable per-token economics with reserved capacity. Interested primarily in Redwood Dedicated + Optimize recommendations (batching/caching/quantization) but needs private VPC and KMS integration for regulatory reasons.

Requirements (from SE call):
- Workloads: real-time chat (avg prompt+context 120 tokens), streaming responses, reranking microservice (short inputs, high QPS), embeddings for document search (background batch).
- Targets: chat p50 <= 40ms, chat p95 <= 120ms; reranking tail < 15ms; embeddings throughput 10k qps batch window.
- Cost: try to keep token unit economics < 0.0008 USD/token effective (after batching/kv cache).
- Model prefs: openquant-7b/8bit quant variants for low-latency produ...

title: Helmcrest RetainWorks
preview: Account summary: mid-market retention SaaS focused on lifecycle messaging and churn prediction. Primary product 'RetainBot' — customer success automation + playbooks. Interested in Redwood because of predictable unit economics and plan to move from Hosted API (fast onboarding) to Dedicated capacity for predictable t...
text:
Account summary: mid-market retention SaaS focused on lifecycle messaging and churn prediction. Primary product 'RetainBot' — customer success automation + playbooks. Interested in Redwood because of predictable unit economics and plan to move from Hosted API (fast onboarding) to Dedicated capacity for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Use-case & acceptance criteria:
- Embeddings-based semantic search over customer help docs and transcripts (1M resources) — target recall > 0.88 and 95th pct latency < 400ms.
- Reranking layer for candidate responses (rerank top 25) to improve NDCG for support suggestions.
- Chat assistant for agents: latency budget 200-300ms p50, cost target to be < $0.10 per 1k active users/month token costs.

POC scope & metrics:
- Phase 1 (Hosted API): validate model quality vs baseline (OpenAI gpt-4-mini) on in-house eval set; measure tokens/response, p50...
